actual_coverage.reasoning: Both probe multi-year durability and drivers of growth despite near-term headwinds (fuel/JVs), similar to assessing the 20% EPS CAGR trajectory; less focus on pre-pandemic comparison in the prediction.
Predicted asks about Q2 yield cadence and offsets; the actual specifically seeks confidence in a Q4 rebound to achieve the FY midpoint, which the prediction does not address.
Both target remaining inventory/booking position and close-in vs forward dynamics amid Mediterranean moderation; the actual additionally asks about conflict scenarios.
Both seek where bookings stand now vs earlier, close-in vs forward mix, and whether Mediterranean weakness is rebounding; prediction does not explicitly cover bookings beyond Q3/into 2027.
Direct match on Perfect Day Mexico ramp cadence and structural yield uplift in the Western Caribbean; Galveston penetration is an added angle but core target is the same.
Prediction asks about capex and cost placement for Perfect Day Mexico; the actual asks specifically whether construction has resumed after an environmental pause.
Prediction decomposes FY Net Yield and the Med impact; the actual asks if this year's European yield shortfall is expected to fully normalize in 2027.
Direct match: yield decomposition by like-for-like vs new hardware/destinations and regional (including Europe) contribution.
Prediction focuses on fuel hedging/sensitivity and NCC ex-fuel structure; the actual asks about itinerary changes due to higher fuel and potential deferral of initiatives.
Predicted asks when NCC ex-fuel normalizes to 2%-3%, aligned with the second part of the actual; it does not cover the hedging posture question, but captures a core intent.
Both target how higher repeat/pre-cruise monetization and cohort behavior impact APD/onboard and net yield; the actual also emphasizes card/loyalty program changes.
No prediction addresses North American airfare inflation impacts on demand/behavior.

Share repurchases vs deleveraging and broader capital-allocation frameworks/dividend triggers

summary: Coverage was solid on key topics like yield decomposition, Mediterranean/booking dynamics, Perfect Day Mexico ramp, and cost normalization/fuel hedging, yielding 7/12 actuals covered (58%). Precision was moderate (48/109 useful), with many overpredictions on capital structure, JV, and ship-platform economics that did not appear in the actual Q&A. Gaps included specificity on Q4 yield rebound confidence, construction status at Perfect Day Mexico, potential 2027 recapture of European yields, itinerary changes due to fuel, and airfare-driven demand effects.
